summary: Human teeth are adapted for eating both animals and plants but show no specific adaptation for one over another; they have characteristics typical of omnivory including sharp canine teeth as well as flat molars useful for crushing food like grains or vegetables. The presence of multiple types of teeth indicates versatility necessary for consuming various dietary items commonly found within mixed ecosystems where different resources might be available seasonally or geographically. Thus, it supports classification under B) Omnivore category based primarily upon their diverse functional morphology aligned across species known to consume such varied fare without specialized
